page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-4-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core. The instance does not include GPU accelerators, and the local storage size is specified as None. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The c4a-standard-4-lssd belongs to the c4a server family, utilizing a dedicated CPU allocation model. This ensures predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a standard resource density profile, offering a qualitative balance between compute capacity and memory footprint for workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or local NVMe storage.

This server configuration is designed for general-purpose workloads. Suitable use cases include mid-tier web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and small-scale database hosting where dedicated CPU execution is required but massive memory arrays or local SSD performance are not necessary.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-4-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Part of the c4a server family, this instance features a dedicated CPU allocation model to ensure consistent compute performance without resource sharing. The instance does not include GPU hardware, and local storage is not provisioned. With its standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the server provides a predictable resource profile for general-purpose applications. It is designed for workloads such as web serving, containerized microservices, and application hosting that require dedicated processing power without the need for specialized memory-optimized or storage-optimized hardware.
